title: teacher_evidence_dashboard
description: Defines what a teacher can see about student performance in a game and how fast they can interpret it. Written as a spec, not as live data — it states what MUST be computable and visible for classroom readiness. See docs/os_spec_2026.md Section 4.5 and Approval Gate 7.

session_context.description: When and how the teacher sees this dashboard. e.g. 'after class session', 'live during play', 'weekly review'.

interpretation_speed_target_seconds.description: Maximum time a teacher should need to identify the most confused student or most common misconception. Approval Gate 7 requires this to be achievable. Recommend: 60 seconds or less for classroom-ready status.

misconception_frequency_view.description: How the teacher sees which error types are most common.

visible.description: Is this view implemented or specified?

categories_shown.description: Which of the six error categories are shown in this view.

display_format.description: How the data is shown. e.g. 'frequency bar chart', 'count table', 'heat map', 'percentage label'.

update_trigger.description: When data refreshes. e.g. 'end of session', 'every 5 orders', 'real-time'.

stuck_points_by_level.description: Which level(s) students most commonly fail or stop advancing.

data_shown.description: What is shown per level. e.g. ['average orders to advance', 'timeout rate', 'overshoot rate', 'level reached by end of session'].

display_format.description: How the level data is shown. e.g. 'level funnel', 'table', 'list'.

guessing_detection.description: Identifies students who are likely guessing rather than reasoning. Prevents teachers from over-attributing success to understanding.

signal_used.description: What behavioral signals are used to classify likely-guessing. Must match detection_signal fields in the game's misconception_library_entry. e.g. ['tap interval < 400ms on 3+ consecutive items', 'selection same-type ≥ 70% of orders'].

display_format.description: How guessing-risk students are surfaced. e.g. 'student name list with risk indicator', 'count of at-risk students', 'flag icon on student row'.

improvement_after_feedback.description: Whether students who received targeted feedback improved on the next attempt. This is the core formative assessment signal — feedback that does not change behavior is not working.

measured_as.description: How improvement is operationalized. e.g. 'accuracy rate on first 3 orders after a feedback event vs. 3 orders before', 'reduction in same-error category events'.

display_format.description: How this is shown to the teacher. e.g. 'before/after comparison row', 'trend line per student', 'aggregate improvement rate'.

confusion_by_representation.description: Which representations or problem types cause the most confusion. Helps teachers decide which concept to teach or reteach.

representations_tracked.description: Which distinct representations are tracked separately. e.g. ['degree prompts', 'radian prompts', 'low-value targets (≤8)', 'high-value targets (>10)'].

display_format.description: How representation-specific confusion is shown. e.g. 'accuracy column per representation type', 'confusion heat map'.

approval_gate_7_met.description: Explicit declaration: can a teacher identify the most confused student or most common misconception within interpretation_speed_target_seconds? Set true only when this has been tested with at least one non-designer teacher.

printable_review_tasks.description: Optional. Describes teacher-facing printable resources for follow-up instruction.
